# Task — Resolve Icosian Coordinatisation Inconsistency

**Task type:** Pair-programmer work (codex lead, Claude review).
**Priority:** Blocks O5 / P5 / P5-apex in observer-zeta, and H-grad-1 Round 2.
**Date opened:** 2026-04-23.

---

## §1 The conflict

Two files in `papers/cascade-derivation/` describe the icosian ring `I`
inconsistently. The canonical source lives outside that directory and
agrees with neither fully.

**Canonical source (do NOT edit):**
`papers/cascade-algebraic-substrate/cascade-algebraic-substrate.tex`,
Definition of `I` (~line 480–514) and `rmk:I-rationality` at lines
503–514:

> The icosian ring is *not* of the simple form
> `Z{1, i, j, k} ⊕ Zφ·{1, i, j, k}`: that Z-module fails to contain
> the half-integer generators `½(±1 ± i ± j ± k)`. The correct `I` is
> strictly larger and includes such half-integer combinations; this
> is what makes its trace form unimodular and equal to the `E_8`
> form [Conway–Sloane Ch. 8, Eq. (17)]. We rely on this classical
> structure rather than on any naive coordinatization.

`I` has `Z`-rank 8 (Conway–Sloane §8.2, Table 8.1), and is a free
`Z[φ]`-module of rank 4 (since `Z[φ]` is a PID and `I` is a
finitely-generated torsion-free `Z[φ]`-module). Z-basis in
Conway–Sloane Table 8.1; equivalent basis of 8 unit icosians in
Moody–Patera Theorem 4.1.

**Offending file 1:** `papers/cascade-derivation/cascade-12d-closure.md`
lines 35–40, Level 2:

> `Z[φ]` extended by quaternion units i, j, k: the **icosian ring
> I ⊂ H**.
> Rank 4 over `Z[φ]`, rank 8 over `Z`.

The phrase "Z[φ] extended by quaternion units i, j, k" naturally
reads as `Z[φ]·{1, i, j, k}`, which is the wrong lattice. The rank
numbers themselves are correct; the *basis choice* is wrong.

**Offending file 2:** `papers/cascade-derivation/WO-H-GRAD-1.md` §O2
(around line 56):

> The icosian ring is rank 4 over `Z[φ]`, rank 8 over `Z`. The quoted
> basis …

Check the exact phrasing — it may also imply the wrong basis.

---

## §2 What a good resolution looks like

**Primary edit:** Rewrite `cascade-12d-closure.md` Level 2 so it no
longer implies `I = Z[φ]·{1, i, j, k}`. Replace with a description
that:

1. Correctly states `I` is the `Z`-subring of `H` generated by the
   120 unit icosians (binary icosahedral group, `2I`).
2. States `Z`-rank 8 and `Z[φ]`-rank 4 (both correct).
3. Explicitly flags that the `Z[φ]`-basis is *not* `{1, i, j, k}` —
   the lattice includes half-integer generators
   `½(±1 ± i ± j ± k)`.
4. Cites Conway–Sloane Ch. 8 (Table 8.1) *or* points to the
   canonical source `cascade-algebraic-substrate.tex` §(icosian
   definition).

Keep the surrounding narrative of Level 2 (the unit icosians being
the 600-cell vertices, binary icosahedral group, meaning of
permeability closure). Just fix the basis description.

**Downstream propagation (surgical, small):**

- `cascade-observer-zeta.md` §4.2 item **O6** (around line 476):
  mark resolved once the primary edit lands. Update the line to
  something like "**RESOLVED 2026-04-23.** See
  cascade-12d-closure.md Level 2 (canonical reference to
  cascade-algebraic-substrate)." Do NOT silently remove O6 — keep
  the row with resolution status.

- `cascade-observer-zeta-catalogue.md` O6 row (around line 60): same
  resolution treatment.

- `cascade-observer-zeta.md` §4.1 ledger row **C7** (around line
  429): C7 cites `cascade-12d-closure.md:47` for the "naive icosian
  description." After the fix, either (i) the reference is no
  longer "naive" and the downgrade no longer applies (in which case
  upgrade C7's status back), or (ii) the C7 downgrade has an
  unchanged basis (the `dual_600cell_factor2.py` simplified-
  decomposition caveat). Make the right call and update the row
  accordingly. Document your reasoning in-line in the row or in a
  short note.

- `WO-H-GRAD-1.md` §O2 (around line 56): review the phrasing. If it
  implies the naive basis, fix it. If it just states rank numbers,
  leave it.

- `cascade-h-grad-1-closure.md` §2 (already uses the correct
  Hurwitz-style half-integer generators `ω_2`, `ω_3` at lines
  86–87): verify consistency — nothing should need editing here,
  but confirm.

**Catalogue update:** If you create any new definitions or
add any numerical results, add ledger rows to
`cascade-observer-zeta-catalogue.md`. If you merely correct
wording, no ledger row is needed.

---

## §3 Acceptance criteria

A. `cascade-12d-closure.md` Level 2 no longer implies
   `I = Z[φ]·{1, i, j, k}`. It cites Conway–Sloane Table 8.1 or
   `cascade-algebraic-substrate.tex` as source of truth.

B. The rank claims (`Z`-rank 8, `Z[φ]`-rank 4) are preserved, and
   are stated in a form that does NOT conflict with the canonical
   source.

C. The upward-closure narrative in `cascade-12d-closure.md` (Level
   2 feeding into Level 3 and Level 4) still reads coherently.

D. `cascade-observer-zeta.md` O6, C7, and the catalogue O6 row are
   updated to reflect the resolution.

E. `WO-H-GRAD-1.md` is checked for naive-basis phrasing and
   corrected if needed.

F. No edits to `cascade-algebraic-substrate.tex`. It is already
   correct.

G. No "publication ready" declarations. This is a surgical
   consistency fix, not a full review pass.

---

## §4 Scope guardrails

- Do NOT rewrite `cascade-12d-closure.md` wholesale. Make minimal
  surgical edits to Level 2 and any other affected paragraph.
- Do NOT change the 12D closure argument itself. The argument is
  about permeability / phason-complement structure and does not
  depend on the icosian-basis choice.
- Do NOT touch `cascade-algebraic-substrate.tex` — it is the source
  of truth for this topic.
- Do NOT mark anything "publication ready" or "resolved in
  principle." The O6 resolution claim is strictly about consistency
  of the basis description.
- If you discover the inconsistency runs deeper than a basis typo
  (e.g., a downstream computation actually used the naive basis and
  produces a wrong numerical result), STOP, report, and do not
  attempt a deeper correction — that becomes a new task.

---

- `papers/cascade-derivation/cascade-h-grad-1-closure.md` §2
  (verify the `ω` basis used there matches Conway–Sloane).

Useful background:
- Conway & Sloane, *Sphere Packings, Lattices, and Groups* (3rd
  ed.), Ch. 8.
- Moody & Patera, *Quasicrystals and Icosians*, J. Phys. A 26
  (1993).

## §6 Reviewer expectations

Claude Code will review by:

1. `git diff` of the working tree.
2. Verifying Acceptance criteria A–G.
3. Firing `scripts/review_wo.sh` on
   `cascade-observer-zeta.md` with focus "Round 6 — Task #10
   icosian-coordinatisation resolution, verify C7/O6 updates are
   consistent with fixed cascade-12d-closure.md Level 2" as
   independent audit.
4. If the review lands clean, task #10 is closed.
